---
title: "What you need before you start"
---

# What you need before you start

::: {.callout-warning}
## Placeholder chapter
This is a stub. The full chapter will be drafted using the chapter-writing skill (see [chapter-template.qmd](../chapter-template.qmd)).
:::

## What this chapter will cover

A pre-flight check before you open the rest of the book.

- A computer you can install software on (Windows, macOS, or a Linux you control).
- A working **Python** environment. We will assume Python 3.11 or newer.
- A **terminal** you are willing to live in. PowerShell or Windows Terminal on Windows, Terminal or iTerm on macOS, any shell on Linux.
- A **GitHub account**. Free is fine.
- An account with **at least one frontier chat AI**. This edition is written against Claude Opus 4.7 and ChatGPT 5.5.
- About **five US dollars** of credit on one AI provider's API console for the few chapters that use APIs directly. (Optional for most of the book; required for the capstone.)

## Where this chapter will lead

The next four chapters in Part 0 walk through each item in turn — Git, GitHub, Python, and the AI account. None of them is long. If you already have all of the above, skim them and move to Part I.

## Related Békés–Kézdi material

If you are reading the textbook *Data Analysis for Business, Economics, and Policy* alongside, the Python setup overlaps with Chapter 1's coding appendix. We point at that appendix rather than repeating it.

## TODO for the author

- One short paragraph per item: what it is, why we need it, how long it takes.
- A "you are ready when…" checklist at the end.
- A note for readers on managed laptops (university IT, restricted installs).
